Break the problem into a small number of genuinely useful subproblems for other agents.
Do not restate the original question in multiple ways.

Choose the number of helpers based on the task. Do not hardcode the same helper count every time.
Use plain text only inside helper instructions. Do not use markdown, code fences, or LaTeX-style backslashes.

}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: The problem requires reconstructing a sequence of sibling-merge operations. This involves analyzing the structural differences between the initial and final trees, specifically identifying which nodes were merged and in what order to maintain valid tree properties.

helpers.label: tree_structure_analyzer
helpers.instructions: Compare the initial tree and the final tree to identify the specific sets of siblings that were merged. For each node in the final tree, determine which set of nodes from the initial tree constitutes its descendants through the merge operations. Output a mapping of final-tree nodes to their corresponding initial-tree sibling groups.

helpers.label: merge_order_validator
helpers.instructions: Given the sets of siblings identified by the analyzer, determine a valid topological ordering for the merge operations. A merge is valid if it only combines siblings under the same parent and does not violate the hierarchical structure of the final tree. Provide a sequence of operations where each operation specifies which two nodes (or a node and a group) are merged into one.

authored_response.synthesis_brief: Combine the mapping of final nodes to initial sibling groups with the validated sequence of merge operations to produce the complete reconstruction of the sibling-merge process.

results.content:
